title: Inequality
description: Practice what you've learned about he sources of income inequality, using the Gini coefficient as a measure of income inequality, and interpreting the Gini coefficient.
topic_path: Market failure and the role of government

assessment_items.question_content:
**What relationship does the Lorenz curve illustrate?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the distribution of income within a population
the relationship between income and consumption
the total amount of production of two goods that is possible given current resources
the hypothetical relationship between the degree of income inequality in a population and per capita income
the hypothetical relationship between tax rates and tax revenues

assessment_items.question_content:
Priya is conducting research to examine the degree of income inequality in her city. 

**Which of these measures is most relevant to her research?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the Lorenz curve
the Laffer curve
the yield curve
the Phillips curve
the J-curve

assessment_items.question_content:
**If a researcher wants to compare income inequality between countries, which measure should she use?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the Gini coefficient
income elasticity of demand
deadweight loss
the correlation coefficient
consumption function

assessment_items.question_content:
The nation of Maxistan has a Gini coefficient of $0.25$ and the nation of Hamsterville has a Gini coefficient of $0.4$.

**Which of the following statements is definitely true?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Hamsterville has higher income inequality than Maxistan.
$40\%$ of Hamsterville’s population has income below the poverty line
$25\%$ of income in Maxistan is saved
Hamsterville’s income per capita is higher than Maxistan’s income per capita
Maxistan’s per capita income is higher than Hamsterville’s per capita income

assessment_items.question_content:
**What does it mean if a country has a Gini coefficient of $0$?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: everyone has the same income
everyone has the same wealth
one person earns all income
one person holds all wealth
the economy is growing

assessment_items.question_content:
The Gini coefficients for five countries are shown in this table.

Country | Gini coefficient
-|-
A | $0.10$
B | $0.25$
C | $0.50$
D | $0.75$
E | $0.80$

**Which of the following statements must be true based on these Gini coefficients?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Income inequality is lowest in country A and highest in country E.
Country C has perfect income equality.
Three times as many people in country D live below the poverty line as in country B.
80% of the population in country D live above the poverty line.
National income is highest in country A.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of these Gini coefficients indicates the smallest amount of income inequality?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

The Lorenz curves for Lincolnland before and after a policy change is shown in this graph. 



![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/ab0e4b2ce155b8655932f09007362c8f.png)

**Which of the following policy changes would most likely lead to the change shown?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: A switch from a flat tax to a progressive tax system
A switch to a regressive tax system
Redistribution of wealth from labor to owners of capital
A switch to a flat tax system
Restricting access to financial markets

assessment_items.question_content:
This graph shows the Lorenz curves for five countries.

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/293f2695fb5fdac109ef192d8285f20d.png)

**Which country has the least income inequality?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Country 1
Country 2
Country 3
Country 4
Country 5

assessment_items.question_content:
The Lorenz curves for Country A and Country B are shown in this graph.



![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/fecd2a61481be078b446dc81112c04bb.png)

**What conclusion does this graph support?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: There is more income inequality in country B than in country A.
The Gini coefficient for country A is greater than for country B.
Per capita income is higher in country A than in country B
Per capita income is higher in country B than in country A
Taxes are higher in country A than in Country B

assessment_items.question_content:
**What happens to the Gini coefficient and the Lorenz curve if an economy implements a progressive tax structure?**


[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The Gini coefficient decreases; the Lorenz curve moves closer to the line of equality.
The Gini coefficient is unaffected, the Lorenz curve moves closer to the line of equality.
The Gini coefficient increases, the Lorenz curve moves farther away from the line of equality.
The Gini coefficient decreases; the Lorenz curve is unaffected
The Gini coefficient decreases, the Lorenz curve moves farther away from the line of equality.

assessment_items.question_content:
The Council of Advisors in the nation of Johnsrudia has come up with a list of possible policy actions for the coming year. 

**Which of these policy actions would be most likely to lead to a less equal distribution of income?**


[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: a regressive tax structure
a progressive tax structure
expanding access to financial markets to all citizens
reducing discrimination in labor markets
improving access to human capital

assessment_items.question_content:
The Gini coefficient in Burginville has been increasing every year for the past 10 years. 

**Which of the following is the most likely cause of a higher Gini coefficient?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: increasing marginal revenue product of capital
switching from a regressive tax system to a progressive tax system
expanding access to capital markets
improving mobility between different job markets
Improving access to social networks

assessment_items.question_content:
The Gini coefficient in Hamsterville has decreased every year for the past ten years. 

**Which of the following is a potential cause of a decreasing Gini coefficient?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the implementation of a progressive income tax system
the switch to a tax system based on sales taxes
restrictions on the ability of labor unions to negotiate
increasing the returns to owners of capital
a stock market boom that benefited the nation’s top 10% of income earners
